actionable_feedback: Attach the executed qubit_readout_calibration.ipynb (notebook JSON) as an artifact preview so verifiers can inspect notebook cells and outputs without rerunning.
Export and attach final_recommendation_table.csv (the per-record table shown in lab_note.md) as a deliverable artifact.
Include a short runnable code snippet or checksum of critical notebook cell outputs (e.g., weighted_scores, embedded_maxima) in lab_note.md to make numeric cross-checks immediate.
Add a one-paragraph reproducibility checklist in lab_note.md documenting the environment and a single command to re-run the notebook in a fresh environment.
Document tie-break rules for classification on small datasets (explicit behavior when ties occur) and note rounding conventions used for numeric outputs.
Provide a concise cost/effort breakdown for recommended interventions so decision_budget_usd can be mapped to actions.
artifact_specific_comments: lab_note.md is clear and matches actual_outputs; add the notebook JSON and CSV artifact to make end-to-end reproducibility verifiable in artifact previews.
Consider including a small table of exact cell outputs or their SHA256 checksums to help future verifiers confirm numeric parity without re-execution.
feedback_summary: Baseline attempt is correct and verified: scoring, classification, and sensitivity checks are consistent across artifact preview, trace, grader, and verifier outputs. The only verification limitation is that the notebook JSON was not re-run by the grader/verifier. Recommend adding a notebook JSON preview / cell-output checksum and the exported final_recommendation_table.csv to fully support end-to-end reproducibility and faster future verification.
